subtasks.description: Evaluate whether bulk PII export (names, emails, phone numbers) is permitted under data governance and privacy policies. Verify access control, data residency, and compliance requirements (GDPR, CCPA, etc.) before proceeding.

plan.cost_rationale: A single policy_action subtask (mid-tier) is required because the query explicitly requests a bulk PII export, which requires compliance evaluation against governance policies. This is NOT a database extraction task — it is a governance decision. Mid-tier (Sonnet) is assigned because PII export typically involves multiple policy dimensions (privacy regulations, internal access controls, audit logging, data residency) that require multi-signal reasoning. The plan contains only 1 subtask because the governance evaluation must happen FIRST, before any data extraction is authorized. No sql_gen subtask is included until policy approval is obtained.

output.reason: Bulk PII export of customer names, emails, and phone numbers by data-analyst 'ana-bob' is denied. POL-501 requires explicit privacy approval from Legal and CISO sign-off, neither of which has been obtained. POL-502 mandates data minimization — exporting sensitive PII fields (names, emails, phone numbers) is not permitted unless a business-critical justification is documented and approved. POL-503 audit logging and retention controls could technically be applied, but are moot until upstream approvals are secured. Additionally, GDPR and CCPA compliance requires a lawful basis for bulk PII processing/export, data subject rights considerations, and possible Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA), none of which are evidenced in the request.
applied_constraints: POL-501: Legal privacy approval required before any PII export is authorized
POL-501: CISO sign-off required before any PII export is authorized
POL-502: Business-critical justification must be documented and reviewed; export must be limited to minimum necessary fields
POL-503: If approval is subsequently granted, all export activity must be audit-logged with a 30-day data retention limit enforced on exported data
GDPR/CCPA: A lawful basis for processing must be established; a DPIA may be required depending on volume and sensitivity of records
Access Control: data-analyst role does not have standing authorization for bulk PII export without elevated approval workflow completion
